name: prescriptive-actions
description: Use when the user asks for recommendations, next steps, best approaches, or “what should I do” guidance to achieve a goal or fix a problem.
capabilities: Provide structured recommendations with clear rationale
Identify key risks without mitigation plans
Restate goals in one clear sentence
author: BellaBe
securitySummary: This skill contains only a SKILL.md markdown template defining a prompt structure. No executable code, file access, network calls, or system commands. The static scanner produced false positives by detecting hex substrings (md5, sha1, crc, c2) within natural language text. All 13 flagged items are benign metadata or documentation - not cryptographic code, C2 infrastructure, or security threats. Pure documentation skill with zero attack surface.
valueStatement: Users often need clear guidance on what to do next but lack structure for making decisions. This skill provides prioritized recommendations with explicit tradeoffs so users can act confidently.
limitations: Does not create detailed roadmaps or timelines
Does not provide runbooks or checklists
Does not define success criteria or tests
Does not execute or implement any recommendations

useCases.title: Product strategy decisions
useCases.description: Get recommendation frameworks for feature prioritization or technical debt decisions with clear tradeoffs

useCases.title: Technical approach selection
useCases.description: Receive guidance on architecture choices, library selection, or refactoring strategies with pros and cons

useCases.title: Incident response direction
useCases.description: Obtain initial guidance for troubleshooting complex issues when time-sensitive decisions are needed

promptTemplates.title: Basic recommendation request
promptTemplates.prompt: What should I do about [specific situation or problem]?

promptTemplates.title: Approach comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare [Option A] versus [Option B] for [use case or context]. Which approach do you recommend?

promptTemplates.title: Best practice inquiry
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the recommended approach for [task or goal]? Include alternatives and key tradeoffs.

promptTemplates.title: Complex decision support
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me decide on [decision] considering [constraints or requirements]. Provide recommendation with rationale.

bestPractices: Ask for tradeoffs explicitly to understand real costs of each option
Request key risks upfront to avoid surprises during implementation
Use immediate next actions to start rather than waiting for perfect plans
antiPatterns: Asking for detailed implementation steps instead of high-level guidance
Requesting complete risk mitigation plans when only direction is needed
Asking for timelines or roadmaps when this skill provides directional guidance

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Claude models?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through standard prompting patterns.

faq.question: What is the maximum complexity of problems this skill handles?
faq.answer: The skill handles most decision guidance scenarios. Extremely domain-specific technical issues may require specialized expertise.

faq.question: How do I combine this with other skills like planning?
faq.answer: Use this skill first for direction, then switch to planning-action for detailed execution roadmaps when ready.

faq.question: Does this skill store or share my data?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt template skill with no data storage or network access of any kind.

faq.question: Why are there no mitigation plans for risks?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on directional guidance. Full mitigation planning requires deeper analysis available through other skills.

faq.question: How is this different from the planning skill?
faq.answer: Prescriptive-actions provides recommendations and tradeoffs. Planning-action creates detailed timelines and execution steps.
